mechanism_narrative: A low and stable FEDFUNDS path anchors front-end rates and supports risk appetite, so the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days when monetary policy is easing or held below recent inflation pressure. If policy rates stop falling or CPI re-accelerates, the signal fails because the market shifts to higher terminal-rate pricing.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS is unchanged or rising while 20-day yields still fall in most triggered windows, or if CPIAUCSL acceleration coincides with positive yield moves less than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises sharply from an already elevated level, growth fears dominate and Treasury buying pushes the 10-year yield down over the next 5 trading days. The effect should vanish if unemployment is flat or if labor data improve instead of deteriorate.
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward yields do not fall after UNRATE crosses the threshold, or if the average yield change is positive in triggered observations despite PAYEMS declining.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS and HOUST signals broad nominal demand strength, which raises inflation expectations and term premia and pushes the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when retail sales improve without housing strength, because the demand signal is then too narrow to lift long rates.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 20-day yield changes are not concentrated in periods when both RSAFS and HOUST rise together, or if the interaction term adds no predictive power versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent gains in INDPRO indicate stronger supply-side utilization and reduced slack, which lift real-rate expectations and should move the 10-year yield up over a 60-day horizon. If industrial production is rising while unemployment is also rising, the signal fails because the expansion is not broad enough to reprice longer bonds.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day yield changes are not positive when INDPRO trends up for two consecutive months, or if the sign flips negative whenever UNRATE is rising alongside INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: If GDPC1 growth outpaces labor-market improvement, the market infers firmer real activity and higher nominal growth, which should raise the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days. The claim is falsified when GDP strength is accompanied by weak payroll growth, because then the growth signal is discounted as temporary or backward-looking.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > prior quarter and PAYEMS 3m change > 0
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce positive 20-day yields more often than not, or if the interaction loses significance after controlling for either GDP or payroll growth alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained gap where FEDFUNDS exceeds CPIAUCSL inflation momentum tightens real policy conditions and should push the 10-year yield down over the next 20 trading days through slower growth and flatter inflation expectations. The signal fails if inflation is accelerating faster than policy tightening, because then nominal yields rise instead.
failure_condition: Reject if the yield does not fall after policy-inflation spread widens, or if the sign is positive in most windows when FEDFUNDS is above inflation trend.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate with continuing payroll growth signals a still-tight labor market, which should lift expected inflation persistence and raise the 10-year yield over the next 5 trading days. The mechanism breaks when unemployment is low but payrolls contract, because the market then reads the labor market as turning weaker rather than tight.
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day yields are not positive in the majority of threshold episodes, or if the relationship reverses when PAYEMS growth turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising HOUST together with stable or improving RSAFS indicates demand normalization and higher future nominal growth, which should push the 10-year yield up over 60 trading days. The signal fails if housing improves without retail-sales follow-through, because the recovery is then too narrow to alter long-rate pricing.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day yields fail to rise when both housing starts and retail sales improve, or if the combined signal underperforms either series by itself in walk-forward tests.
